notes: Adult-directed speech overheard by infants. Decreases with age; 2-3x more speech from women than men.

3.6 min/hr TCDS reported (children directly addressed). Conversion: 3.6 min × 60 s × (1 token / 0.3 s) = 720 tokens/hr (using Coffey 2024 / Brent corpus rate).
Reported as similar to Tseltal -- 'surprisingly similar, and relatively infrequent rates of directed input' (cited from this paper's abstract / Bunce 2024).
Lower bound across SES groups. Effect of SES g=0.41 (statistically significant but smaller than HR's 30M-word-gap claim).
Upper bound across SES groups. SES effect on input quantity moderated by what counts as 'language' (CDS-only vs all-speech).
From Coffey et al. 2024 reading: 1200 tok/hr CDS used in 'standard model' calculations. Original Kachergis et al. paper is at papers/kachergis_etal_2022_jslhr.pdf.

~1 hr/year of 'optimal' CDS at 8h/day waking = 1 hr × 200 words/min × 60 / (8 × 365) ≈ 4 tokens per waking hour. Most restrictive filters (Pirahã CDS rate ×0.3 mother ×0.3 noise-free ×0.9 no-overlap). Coffey Table 1 row 'Optimal min'.

5 cultural groups studied: NA English, UK English, Argentinian Spanish, Tseltal, Yélî Dnye. TCDS rates stable across early development; ADS decreases with age. Children consistently hear more ADS than TCDS.
From Coffey et al. 2024 reading: 1800 tok/hr overheard speech.
Per-recording adult speech rate (re-derived from data/raw_data/seedlings/lena_data.csv; same children sampled at 6-7 mo).

40,000+ hours / 2,500 day-long recordings. Adult talk rate is a predictor variable in their model. β=0.26 (log-vocs/hr-of-child-speech) -> log-scale variability across corpora is substantial. SES (maternal education) NOT a significant predictor of child speech in this large sample. Per-corpus AWC distributions reported in supplement.
Studies that measured all speech in environment found no SES effect — supports Sperry et al. 2019's CDS-vs-overheard argument.
